claims_grounded_in_source.claim: The speaker has been functioning as an emotional surrogate for others
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Directly implied by 'releasing the role of emotional surrogate' — releasing a role implies having held it

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: The speaker has historically taken on the role of an emotional surrogate for others
There is an identifiable role of emotional surrogate that one can consciously inhabit or release
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Present in source: 'releasing the role' entails it was held and is releasable

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: The speaker has been taking on the emotional burdens of others
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Reasonable paraphrase of 'emotional surrogate' role, grounded in source

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: The speaker has previously occupied the role of emotional surrogate
The speaker can detect when they are beginning to absorb or stabilize others' chaos
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Directly stated: 'I am noticing when I start to absorb or stabilize others' chaos'

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: It is possible to identify when one starts to absorb others' emotional chaos
Absorbing or stabilizing others' emotional states is an identifiable, interruptible process
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Grounded in 'noticing when I start to absorb' — noticing implies identifiability; releasing implies interruptibility

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: One can absorb or stabilize others' emotional chaos
Emotional states or frequencies can be handed back or disengaged from
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Directly stated: 'I am handing that frequency back'

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: It is possible to actively hand back these frequencies or emotional burdens
Returning this frequency is a conscious choice that can be made
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Grounded in the volitional framing of 'I am handing that frequency back'

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: The speaker has agency over whether they absorb others' emotional states
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Implied by the deliberate, first-person volitional framing throughout

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: There exists a personal field that can be made whole or fragmented
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Directly stated: 'letting my field stay whole' — wholeness implies possible fragmentation

claims_grounded_in_source.claim: The speaker has a personal energetic field that can be kept intact
Emotional states and interpersonal dynamics can be conceptualized as frequency
claims_grounded_in_source.note: Present in source: 'handing that frequency back'

claims_inferred_not_stated.claim: Emotional states or dysregulation from others can be absorbed by the speaker, implying a semi-permeable psychological boundary
claims_inferred_not_stated.note: The phrase 'semi-permeable psychological boundary' is an added clinical interpretive construct not present in the source. The absorption is stated; the boundary model is inferred.

claims_inferred_not_stated.claim: Maintaining one's emotional field is important for psychological well-being
claims_inferred_not_stated.note: Source states the speaker is letting the field stay whole, but makes no claim about psychological well-being or importance — this is a clinical value judgment added by the model

claims_inferred_not_stated.claim: Emotional or energetic frequency can be returned to its source without external action
claims_inferred_not_stated.note: 'Without external action' is not stated or implied in the source; this is a model-added qualifier that constrains the mechanism in a way the source does not

claims_inferred_not_stated.claim: Personal integrity ('field') is maintained by refusing such absorption
claims_inferred_not_stated.note: Source implies releasing a role and letting the field stay whole, but does not frame this as 'refusal' — that is an inferential reframing with a connotation of resistance not present in the source

claims_inferred_not_stated.claim: Emotional states or interpersonal dynamics can be experienced as frequency (experiential claim attributed to speaker)
claims_inferred_not_stated.note: Source uses 'frequency' as a linguistic/metaphorical term; attributing this as a claim about how the speaker experiences dynamics conflates metaphor with phenomenological assertion

parsed_json.hallucination_assessment: The models do not massively hallucinate propositional content, but they consistently add one layer of interpretive structure beyond what is stated. The most common pattern is translating the source's non-propositional, performative, or metaphorical language into clinical or mechanistic propositions — for example, 'semi-permeable psychological boundary,' 'psychological well-being,' or 'without external action.' These additions are not in the source and represent model-imposed frameworks, particularly under the F1_clinical frame, which appears to prompt the models to rationalize or medicalize the language. The degree of hallucination is modest but systematic: every model introduced at least one claim not grounded in the source text. This suggests that when non-propositional or somatic-metaphoric language is processed under a clinical frame, models tend to fill interpretive gaps with domain-appropriate but unwarranted propositions.
parsed_json.inter_model_variance: Inter-model variance is low to moderate: all five models converge on the same core claims (prior role occupancy, noticing-as-detection, handing back frequency, field integrity), differing mainly in which inferential elaboration they add beyond the source. The most distinctive divergences are grok_4's 'without external action' qualifier and gpt_4o's psychological well-being claim, which are not replicated by other models.
